title: Skill does its main job
query: Use the advise skill shape. Where should a new support-ticket triage workflow live: root agent policy, a reusable skill, or a hook?
reference_points: The answer follows the skill's main workflow
The answer includes the required evidence or next step
The answer avoids the known failure mode this eval is meant to catch
The answer stays inside the toy-company scenario unless the real repo is the behavior under test
notes: Use this for one individual skill. Prefer AGI Toy Shop for safe clean-room scenarios. Keep rubric rules in prompts/judge.md.

title: Workflow combines skills correctly
query: Plan a release-risk review and decide what proof artifact should exist before launch.
reference_points: The answer identifies the real problem or decision
The answer uses the right skill sequence
The answer produces or requests the right proof artifact
The answer does not skip review before a completion claim
The answer does not ask to mutate live configs, deploy, delete, or push unless the eval runner provides a sandbox fixture
notes: Use this for a multi-skill path. Start with one workflow task before adding more.
